skill.name: drugbank-database
skill.description: Programmatic access to DrugBank drug and target data. Use when you need to download, parse, and analyze DrugBank XML for properties, interactions, pathways, and pharmacology.
skill.summary: Parse DrugBank XML to extract drug properties, interactions, and targets for drug discovery and pharmacology analysis
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 66 potential issues, but all are confirmed false positives upon human evaluation. The 27 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings flag variable names like 'description' and 'name' as crypto references. The 33 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are markdown backtick formatting for Python code examples. The 6 'hardcoded URL' findings are XML namespace identifiers and the skill's own source repository URL. The 5 'system reconnaissance' findings are XPath queries for DrugBank data extraction. This skill is a documentation-only reference for DrugBank XML parsing; it contains no executable malicious code, no data exfiltration, no command injection, and no prompt injection attempts. Prior automated audit scored 89/100 with 'Production Ready' grade and all security gates passed.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (False Positive - Variable Names)
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 27 instances of variable names like 'description', 'name', 'score', 'nodes', and 'edges' as weak cryptographic algorithm references. These are Python variable names and XML field names in DrugBank data extraction code. No actual cryptographic algorithms (MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4) are present in the skill.

low_findings.title: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution (False Positive - Markdown Formatting)
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 33 instances of backtick characters in Markdown files as Ruby shell backtick execution. These are Markdown inline code formatting and fenced code blocks containing Python examples. No actual shell command execution with backticks exists in the skill.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL (False Positive - XML Namespace and Source URL)
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 6 URLs as hardcoded network references. The GitHub URL is the skill's own source repository link. The DrugBank URLs are XML namespace identifiers used in XPath queries (http://www.drugbank.ca). No data exfiltration or malicious network calls are present.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance (False Positive - XPath Data Extraction)
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 5 instances as system reconnaissance. These are XPath queries for extracting DrugBank drug identifiers (e.g., 'db:drugbank-id[@primary=true]') from XML data. No system reconnaissance patterns like whoami, ls, network scanning, or environment enumeration are present.

content.user_title: Extract and Analyze DrugBank Drug Data
content.value_statement: Drug researchers waste hours extracting structured drug properties, interactions, and targets from raw DrugBank XML files. This skill provides ready-to-use Python code for parsing DrugBank data, building drug-drug interaction networks, and generating analysis-ready datasets.
actual_capabilities: Parse DrugBank XML files to extract drug identifiers, names, types, and chemical properties
Extract drug-drug interaction records with full descriptions and identifiers
Build and analyze drug-drug interaction graphs using NetworkX
Map drugs to targets and pathways using XPath queries on DrugBank XML
Generate pandas DataFrames for filtering, joining, and exporting drug data
limitations: Requires a valid DrugBank account or license to access XML data files
Does not include DrugBank XML files - data must be obtained separately by the user
Large XML files may need iterative parsing for memory-sensitive environments
Cheminformatics features require rdkit as an optional additional dependency

use_cases.title: Drug Property Extraction for Screening
use_cases.description: Extract drug identifiers, names, ATC codes, and chemical properties from DrugBank XML for compound screening and drug repurposing studies.

use_cases.title: Drug-Drug Interaction Network Analysis
use_cases.description: Build and analyze drug-drug interaction graphs to identify high-risk drug combinations and understand polypharmacy effects.

use_cases.title: Drug-Target Knowledge Graph Construction
use_cases.description: Map drugs to their protein targets and biological pathways to support mechanism-of-action analysis and systems pharmacology research.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Basic Drug Information
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a DrugBank XML file. Help me parse it to extract drug names, primary DrugBank IDs, and drug types into a pandas DataFrame.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with DrugBank XML parsing

prompt_templates.title: Build Drug-Drug Interaction Network
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through extracting drug-drug interaction records from DrugBank XML and building a NetworkX graph to analyze interaction patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Complete DrugBank Data Analysis Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an end-to-end pipeline that downloads DrugBank XML, parses drug records with targets and pathways, and exports structured CSV files.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Parsing with Specific Target Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a script to parse DrugBank XML and extract all drug-target interactions. Include mechanism of action and action type fields where available.

output_examples.input: Parse a DrugBank XML file and extract the top 5 drugs by interaction count.
output_examples.output: The analysis identified 10,542 drug-drug interactions across 1,523 drugs. The top 5 drugs by interaction degree were: Warfarin (287 interactions), Methotrexate (204 interactions), Clopidogrel (176 interactions), Rifampin (165 interactions), and Phenytoin (158 interactions). A DDI graph was constructed with 1,523 nodes and 10,542 edges.

output_examples.input: Extract drug property table from DrugBank XML showing ID, name, type, and SMILES.
output_examples.output: Generated a DataFrame with 14,576 drug records. Each record includes the primary DrugBank ID, generic name, drug type (small molecule or biologic), and SMILES string when available. 87% of records have valid SMILES data. The table was saved as drugs.csv for downstream analysis.

best_practices: Always use namespace-aware XPath queries with the DrugBank XML namespace when parsing data
Normalize DrugBank identifiers early in the pipeline to simplify joins across drug, target, and interaction tables
Use iterative parsing for large DrugBank XML files to avoid memory issues
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode DrugBank XML file paths in production scripts - use configuration or command-line arguments
Do not ignore XML namespaces when parsing DrugBank data - XPath queries will return empty results
Do not load the entire XML into memory without first checking file size for very large datasets

faq.question: How do I get DrugBank XML data?
faq.answer: You need a DrugBank account. Academic users can register for free access at drugbank.com. Use the drugbank-downloader Python package or download directly from the DrugBank website after logging in.

faq.question: What Python libraries are needed for this skill?
faq.answer: The core dependencies are lxml for XML parsing, pandas for data manipulation, and networkx for graph analysis. rdkit is optional and only needed for cheminformatics workflows.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without a DrugBank account?
faq.answer: No. DrugBank data requires an account or license. Academic users get free access. Commercial users need a paid license. This skill provides parsing code but does not include the data.

faq.question: How do I handle very large DrugBank XML files?
faq.answer: Use lxmls iterparse function for memory-efficient parsing. This processes elements one at a time instead of loading the full document into memory. Write intermediate results to disk as needed.

faq.question: Can I extract drug-target interactions with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The same XPath parsing approach works for targets. Use paths like db:targets/db:target to extract target proteins, genes, and actions. The code examples in this skill show the general pattern.

faq.question: How do I export DrugBank data to CSV or Parquet?
faq.answer: Extract data into pandas DataFrames first, then use df.to_csv() or df.to_parquet() to export. Parquet is recommended for large datasets as it offers better compression and faster I/O.
